meaning: adj.两月一次的,双月的n.双月刊
examples: Under the new plan, customers would pay $45 bimonthly, instead of $18 a month – a substantial increase.
I use my bimonthly weigh-in results to get information about my nutrition as well.
For these reasons, I stopped weighing myself every day and switched to a bimonthly weighing schedule instead.
John, executive director of Centre for Education and Communication and editor of Labour File a bimonthly journal on worker issues.
However, it is not clear that moving from annual reviews to quarterly or even bimonthly is sufficient in our dynamic markets.
